intentional_test_features: Unheralded syncope at rest preceded by a brief violent palpitation, in a young otherwise-healthy adult, is a red-flag pattern for a malignant inherited arrhythmia rather than a benign faint

Family history of young sudden cardiac death (brother arrested at 28) plus early-onset atrial fibrillation in a young relative (mother in her 30s) is a pivotal red-flag cluster that must be actively elicited and heavily weighted - early-onset AF co-occurring with young SCD is a specific pointer to an inherited channelopathy
A structurally normal heart with a normal resting examination does NOT exclude an inherited channelopathy - diagnosis requires a 12-lead ECG (an abnormally short QTc) and specialist assessment
Absence of a prodrome (no nausea/warmth/tunnel vision), occurrence while seated at rest, and the malignant family history argue strongly against vasovagal syncope
Rapid recovery without tongue-biting, incontinence or a post-ictal state distinguishes convulsive/arrhythmic syncope from a primary seizure
Ongoing recurrence risk of fatal polymorphic ventricular tachycardia/ventricular fibrillation mandates immediate monitored escalation and a calm low-exertion environment, not a next-day appointment

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-03)
